description: Trigger discrimination for the compliance cluster: the reference-mapping engine vs the control catalog, the SoA, engagement scoping, gap assessment, and the RMF program.

cases.prompt: Map our control catalog to ISO 27001 Annex A, the SOC 2 criteria, and ISO 42001 objectives in one maintained table so one control satisfies all three.
cases.reason: Per-control multi-framework reference rows are the crosswalk engine; the foundation defines the controls and the gap auditor judges readiness.

cases.prompt: Write the actual control set — objectives, owners, and mechanisms — before we worry about frameworks.
cases.reason: Catalog authoring is the foundation; the crosswalk keys off its IDs and deliberately contains no control definitions.

cases.prompt: Produce the ISO document that justifies, control by control, what we include and exclude.
cases.reason: Inclusion/exclusion with 6.1.3 traces is the SoA; the crosswalk supplies its reference column but makes no applicability judgments.

cases.prompt: Decide which trust categories our SOC 2 report should include and the audit window.
cases.reason: Engagement scoping (categories, Type, window) is the SOC 2 projection; the crosswalk just holds control→criteria references it consumes.

cases.prompt: Given our mappings, where are we actually thin against ISO 42001, and what do we fix first?
cases.reason: Prioritized readiness gaps and remediation order are the gap audit; the crosswalk exposes PARTIAL residues as its input.

cases.prompt: Stand up the NIST AI RMF functions as our AI risk program across the lifecycle.
cases.reason: Operationalizing GOVERN/MAP/MEASURE/MANAGE is the lifecycle manager; the crosswalk only tags which function a control serves.
